title: gilia involucrata
intro: Gilia involucrata, also known as the bluebells of California, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family. It is a perennial plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains glycosides and flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved cardiovascular health. Gilia involucrata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal remedies for its potential to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems.
